corrections.location: Judith 16:18, first line after verse 21 marker
corrections.note: Printed text has singular σκώληκα, not plural σκώληκας.
Transcript adds sigma: image reads ἦλθον.
Name appears with acute in print (Ἰουδεὶθ/Ἰουδείθ distinction hard to judge consistently); left unchanged if normalization policy ignores this.
Print has raised point, not full stop.
A line-break space is missing after ταύτας in transcript; print continues directly with ἀνέζευξεν on same sentence. Anchored insertion to preserve mechanics.

corrections.current: ἀνέζευξεν ἕκαστος εἰς τὴν κληρονομίαν αὐτοῦ
corrections.correct: ἀνέζευξεν ἕκαστος εἰς τὴν κληρονομίαν αὐτοῦ
corrections.note: Print appears to have grave in αὐτοῦ? likely already acceptable; if accents normalized, ignore.

corrections.current: αὐτῆς καὶ προσετέθη πρὸς τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ.
corrections.correct: αὐτῆς καὶ προσετέθη πρὸς τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ·
corrections.note: Print has raised point before verse 23.

corrections.current: καὶ ἦν ἡ προβαίνουσα μεγάλη σφόδρα
corrections.correct: καὶ ἦν ἡ προβαίνουσα μεγάλη σφόδρα.
corrections.note: Print has full stop after σφόδρα before next clause.
The ca notation is cramped; likely correct as transcribed.
Greek accents omitted throughout apparatus; likely acceptable normalization.

Apparatus has ἕκαστος with rough breathing/accent; if apparatus is expected unaccented, only missing initial aspiration mark is not reportable. Core reading omits ἕκαστος, not ἐκαστος? image clearly shows ἑκαστος.

corrections.current: απηλθεν]+ εις τον οικον αυτης ℵ
corrections.correct: απηλθεν]+ εις τον οικον αυτης ℵ*
corrections.note: The siglum is ℵ* (not plain ℵ) in the image.
Printed Latin note reads ως? No, image appears to show 'ως sup ras', not 'os sup ras'.

uncertain.note: Several punctuation marks in print are middle dots/raised points; transcript regularizes some as periods. Reported only where clear.
Accentuation of proper names may be normalized in transcription; image shows mixed accents but policy says be conservative.

notes: Running head matches. Main body is largely correct; a few punctuation and one substantive word-form error. Apparatus mostly matches, but there are a few clear transcription issues, especially εκαθαρισθη and ℵ* after απηλθεν.
